summary: Draft aggregate match-progress PR that replaced many `/// #` stubs with C implementations across fighter, stage, item, library, game-mode, menu, player-stat, and trophy-viewer code. The PR body reported 32 matched functions, 11 failed, and 1 in progress, but it was closed unmerged after the author split it into themed PRs #2341 stages, #2342 items, #2343 Kirby, and #2344 misc; the closing note also says `fn_80200460` was excluded as only a 98.2% match.

key_files.role: Kirby copied Sheik/Mewtwo neutral special logic
Kirby copied Jigglypuff Rollout air-start transition
Popo aerial Belay throw animation callback
fighter color overlay reset and hammer glow reapply
Kongo Jungle bridge and barrel-cannon checks
Rainbow Cruise scrolling segment setup and collision callback
Rainbow Cruise ground-vars layout typed from pad bytes
Dream Land Past wind animation selection
Kirby Final Cutter beam item spawn
THPDecComp field typing and added offsets

what_changed: Replaced PR-listed stubs with C implementations for a broad batch of matched functions; slice counts show 39 changed files, 849 additions, and 64 deletions.
Fighter work covered common grab hold setup, Crazy Hand damage animation, Kirby copied neutral-special paths, Popo Belay throw animation, and fighter color overlay reset.

Stage work covered `grAnime_801C83D0`, Corneria world-position calculation, Kongo bridge/barrel logic, Home Run Contest distance scaling, Dream Land Past wind animation selection, and Rainbow Cruise segment setup/collision code.
Item work covered Blastoise/Kamex animation timer logic, Pikachu Thunder collision, Lugia sub-item spawn, Kirby Cutter beam spawn, and Oldkuri/Goomba walking physics.

Miscellaneous work covered THP playback state advancement and `THPDecComp` field typing, post-match award clearing, name-entry animation updates, player attack-stat counters, and trophy viewer camera reset.

Headers were updated away from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` toward concrete signatures such as `void fn_800DA1D8(Fighter_GObj*)`, `s32 fn_8001F06C(THPDecComp*)`, `bool grAnime_801C83D0(...)`, `Item_GObj* it_802D1E8C(...)`, and `void un_80307828(int)`.

The Rainbow Cruise ground-variable padding was partially modeled as `struct grRCruise_SubEntry x3C[3]`; the diff excerpt shows the struct definition added twice, reinforcing that this was draft work that needed cleanup before landing.
The PR was not merged as-is; the author closed it with a note that it was split into themed successor PRs and that `fn_80200460` was excluded because it was a 98.2% match.
smart_moves: The PR body preserved a useful triage table: matched, failed, and in-progress functions were listed with file names and match status.
The author split a risky cross-system draft into focused PRs: #2341 stages, #2342 items, #2343 Kirby, and #2344 misc.
The closing note explicitly excluded `fn_80200460` when it was only a near-match, rather than landing it as a claimed complete match.
Many matches paired implementation with header prototype fixes, reducing future callsite ambiguity and replacing generic `UNK_*` signatures.
Struct/header work supported matching instead of just local code edits, especially `THPDecComp` signed fields and the Rainbow Cruise `x3C` sub-entry layout.
The implementations reused existing engine helpers for motion transitions, item spawning, animation control, collision callbacks, and effect/audio hooks rather than open-coding behavior.
decomp_lessons: Large decomp batches are valuable as staging/triage artifacts but should be split by subsystem before review and merge.
Do not trust an aggregate match list blindly after closure; successor PRs and closing comments can supersede the initial table, as with `fn_80200460`.
Concrete function signatures are often part of the match: return type, parameter type, and signedness changes appeared alongside many successful functions.
Modeling previously opaque struct fields can unlock cleaner matches, but shared headers must be checked for duplicate or draft-only type definitions.
Matching fighter callbacks often requires preserving callback assignments, motion-state flags, `cmd_vars`, and reloaded fighter pointers in the same shape as the original code.
Item spawn functions should follow the project’s `SpawnItem` initialization pattern: kind, position, facing direction, parent objects, damage, velocity, flags, and post-spawn setup calls.
Stage callbacks often become clearer after naming small layout entries and matching them to map IDs, joint IDs, and collision IDs.
Failed-function lists are useful future work queues; this PR recorded weak or unresolved attempts for `grPura_80212FC0`, `grOnett_801E5194`, `InitNameEntryUIState`, `gm_801BAC9C`, and others.
naming_conventions: Fighter functions keep subsystem/character prefixes: `ftCo_` for common fighter code, `ftKb_` for Kirby, `ftCh_` for Crazy Hand, and `ftPp_` for Popo.
Stage functions keep stage prefixes: `grCorneria_`, `grKongo_`, `grRCruise_`, `grOldPupupu_`, `grHomeRun_`, and shared `grAnime_`.
Item functions use item-kind prefixes when known, such as `itKamex_`, `itPikachuthunder_`, and `itOldkuri_`; address-style `it_802...` names remain where semantics are not yet named.
Header comments retain address tags like `/* 0DA1D8 */` while replacing unknown prototypes with typed signatures.
Unknown ground-variable fields remain offset-style names such as `x3C`, `xC8`, `xCC`, `xD0`, and `xD4` until stronger semantic names are justified.
Local helper names in the draft, such as `ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction`, describe transition behavior without renaming exported address-based symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(...)` in many implementations, with sizes including 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, and 40, to preserve stack layout.
Used pragmas for codegen control: `#pragma auto_inline off` and `#pragma global_optimizer off` around `ftCo_800C0134`, and `#pragma dont_inline on` before `mnName_80238A04`.
Introduced small inline helpers such as `ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction` and `fn_80200460_inline` to shape generated code while keeping the exported function body small.
Used local scopes and repeated `GET_FIGHTER`/`getFighter` reloads in fighter functions, a common register-allocation matching tactic visible in the diff.

Adjusted signedness and returns for matching, e.g. `THPDecComp` fields at 0x68/0x6C/0x108/0x10C became `s32`, `fn_8001F06C` returns `s32`, and several callbacks return `bool` or `void` instead of `UNK_RET`.
Preserved control-flow shapes such as the `goto end` cleanup in `fn_8001F06C` around `OSDisableInterrupts`/`OSRestoreInterrupts`.
Used exact constants and float forms in matched logic, including `0.0F`, `1.0F`, `0.0001f`, `0.017453292F`, `0.3280969F`, `100.0F`, and `M_PI_2`.
Used typed or anonymous local asset-data layouts and explicit offset casts in stage code, especially `grCn_Data` and `grCn_Entry` for Corneria.
review_feedback: There were no review comments or submitted reviews in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot posted a GALE01 report saying `No changes`.
The substantive human follow-up was the author’s closing comment: `Split into themed PRs: #2341 (stages), #2342 (items), #2343 (Kirby), #2344 (misc). Excluded fn_80200460 (98.2% match).`
Because the PR was closed unmerged, future knowledge should prefer the successor themed PRs for final landed code.
